title: Executive Order 12434—Alaska Railroad Rates

flags.justification: Sec. 1 explicitly subjects the Secretary of Transportation's rate-setting authority to the independent Interstate Commerce Commission's authority — a genuine, substantive external check retained rather than bypassed.
Rates must be filed with the ICC 'to the same extent as rates are filed by comparable rail carriers' — a real, established regulatory filing and review process.
Single coherent purpose: establishing Alaska Railroad rate-setting procedure.

Rates must be set 'to the same extent as comparable rail carriers subject to the jurisdiction of the Interstate Commerce Commission' — an objective, externally-anchored benchmark, not a self-defined standard.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
A standing rate-setting procedural framework, evergreen by nature; supersedes a prior order (EO 11107), evidencing periodic revision.
This preserves and formalizes the ICC's oversight role over Alaska Railroad rates rather than removing it.

Sec. 2(b) requires that any filed contract be made available to other shippers under similar conditions — a genuine, structural non-discrimination and transparency requirement protecting competing third parties.
A routine, narrow, technical rate-setting-procedure delegation; no novel authority claim.
The Secretary of Transportation and ICC roles are clean and cooperative, not competing.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/18 = 0.0%. A genuinely well-designed delegation, notable for a design feature not previously seen in this corpus: it explicitly preserves an independent regulatory agency's (the ICC's) oversight over the delegated rate-setting authority rather than creating a parallel or unchecked structure, combined with a real shipper non-discrimination requirement.
